marketFocus: US-Iran ceasefire holding firmly. VP Vance confirmed 'ceasefire is holding and Iranian negotiators want a deal.' A second round of face-to-face talks is expected Thursday or early next week in Pakistan. Trump said the war is 'very close to over.' SPX +1.17%, NDX +1.81%, European equities +0.9–1.4%. DXY pressured as risk-on conditions persist — EUR/USD completely wiped out all war-related losses (since March 2nd) to make a session high of 1.1807.

NZD was the G10 outperformer. ANZ revised its RBNZ outlook to hikes in July, September and October — ANZ Chief Economist said it was 'fair to interpret recent media appearances from Governor Breman as deliberate.' USD March PPI came in at +0.5% M/M vs 1.2% forecast — a significant miss. Fed's Goolsbee warned rate cuts may be delayed until 2027 if the Iran war prolongs. BoJ is reportedly considering a sharp increase to its inflation forecasts this month.

Today's key events: Fed Beige Book, ECB Lagarde/Cipollone/Schnabel speak, BoE Bailey, SNB Schlegel, RBA Hauser, RBNZ Breman. Earnings: Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Hermes. AUD Employment + Unemployment Rate tomorrow (Thu 03:30 FFT) — TONIGHT badge activates from 16:00 FFT today.

USD.tone: DXY Pressured — PPI Miss Seals Dollar Weakness; Goolsbee Cautious on Rate Cuts
USD.summary: USD is Bearish today. The dollar is under sustained pressure after March PPI came in at +0.5% M/M vs the 1.2% forecast — a significant miss that removes a key inflation argument for USD bulls. DXY got only slight reprieve overnight after weakening sharply on Tuesday. Fed's Goolsbee warned that a prolonged Iran war reduces chances of rate cuts and 'may have to wait until 2027' — a cautious tone that does not support USD. The risk-on environment driven by US-Iran peace talk progress continues to weigh on the dollar. Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation hearing is scheduled for next Tuesday April 21st. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: March PPI +0.5% M/M vs 1.2% exp — significant miss removes inflation argument for USD bulls
DXY only slight reprieve after sharp Tuesday decline — structural selling pressure
Fed Goolsbee: rate cuts may be delayed until 2027 if Iran war prolongs — cautious tone
Risk-on environment from US-Iran peace talk progress continues to weigh on dollar
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

EUR.tone: EUR/USD Above 1.1800 — Wiped All War Losses; ECB Lagarde, Schnabel, Cipollone Speak Today
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. EUR/USD completely wiped out all war-related losses (since March 2nd) to make a session high of 1.1807 — a significant technical milestone. Bund futures held gains, set to close firmer by ~30 ticks. ECB's Lagarde said 'it would be a mistake to say we need to look through the shock' and that it is 'too soon to make such a conclusion' — a hawkish lean that supports EUR. ECB's Dolenc said the ECB will raise rates if inflation has a longer-lasting effect. German Wholesale Prices YoY jumped to 4.1% (prev. 1.2%) — above-expected, driven by energy and raw materials. Lagarde, Schnabel and Cipollone all speak today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: EUR/USD above 1.1807 — wiped all war-related losses since March 2nd, key technical milestone
ECB Lagarde: 'mistake to look through the shock' — hawkish lean supports EUR
ECB Dolenc: will raise rates if inflation has longer-lasting effect
German Wholesale Prices YoY 4.1% (prev. 1.2%) — energy/raw materials driving inflation
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

GBP.tone: GBP Near 1.3600 — BoE Mann Hawkish on Wages; BRC Retail Sales Beat; Bailey Speaks Today
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. GBP/USD attempted to breach 1.3600 but fell just short at 1.3589 — a strong session performance. BoE's Mann said she is 'concerned that a price shock could show up in wage expectations' and that 'inflation expectations are very volatile if CPI is above 3–3.5%' — a hawkish signal that supports GBP. UK BRC Retail Sales YoY (Mar) beat strongly at 3.1% vs 0.9% expected, with the firm noting much of the momentum was associated with warmer weather and Easter spending. UK got a record £148bln investor bids for its 10-year debt sale. BoE Governor Bailey speaks today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: GBP/USD near 1.3600 (session high 1.3589) — strong risk-on performance
BoE Mann: 'concerned price shock could show up in wage expectations' — hawkish signal
UK BRC Retail Sales YoY 3.1% vs 0.9% exp — strong beat supports BOE hawkish hold
UK record £148bln investor bids for 10yr debt sale — strong demand for Gilts
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

JPY.tone: JPY Buoyed — BoJ to Sharply Raise Inflation Forecasts; USD/JPY Trickling to 158.63
JPY.summary: JPY is Bullish today. The yen is being buoyed by two powerful drivers: (1) BoJ is reportedly considering a sharp increase to its inflation forecasts this month, while weighing a possible growth outlook cut — a hawkish shift that markets are pricing in; (2) softening crude prices reduce Japan's import cost burden, improving the trade balance outlook. USD/JPY gradually trickled lower throughout the London session to mark a session low of 158.63. Japanese Machinery Orders MM (Feb) came in at 13.6% vs -1.1% expected — a massive beat. Note: JPY Daily Bullish vs Weekly Bearish = ❌ NO alignment — excluded from High Conviction Setups.
drivers: BoJ reportedly considering sharp increase to inflation forecasts this month — hawkish shift
USD/JPY session low 158.63 — yen gradually strengthening through London session
Japanese Machinery Orders MM (Feb) 13.6% vs -1.1% exp — massive beat supports JPY
Softening crude prices reduce Japan's import burden — trade balance tailwind
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment — intraday only

AUD.tone: Antipodeans Outperform — RBA Hauser Hawkish; AUD Employment Tomorrow 03:30 FFT
AUD.summary: AUD is Bullish today. Antipodeans performed well throughout the London session given the risk-on tone. RBA's Hauser speaks today — his recent hawkish lean ('not sure rates are at the right level to tame inflation') continues to support AUD. ASX 200 eked mild gains with strength in tech, gold miners and health care. The PBoC announced CNY 500bln of 183-day outright reverse repos — a significant liquidity injection that supports risk appetite and China-linked currencies. Key risk: AUD Employment Change + Unemployment Rate tomorrow at 03:30 FFT — TONIGHT badge activates from 16:00 FFT today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: Antipodeans outperform in risk-on London session — dual tailwind with RBNZ repricing
RBA Hauser speaks today — hawkish lean on inflation supports AUD
PBoC CNY 500bln 183-day reverse repos — significant liquidity injection supports China-linked AUD
AUD Employment + Unemployment Rate Thu 03:30 FFT — TONIGHT badge from 16:00 FFT today
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

CAD.tone: Oil Below $99 — IEA Cuts Demand Forecast; Energy Sector Lagged; Petro-Proxy Headwind
CAD.summary: CAD is Bearish today. Oil has softened significantly — Brent June fell below $99/bbl (range $96.50–$99.45) and WTI June traded within $90.19–$92.66. The IEA cut its world oil demand forecast by 80k BPD for 2026 due to the Iran conflict and sees world oil supply falling by 1.5mln BPD. The energy sector was the clear laggard in equity markets. US crude private inventories showed a surprise build of +6.1mln barrels vs -1.3mln expected — bearish for oil and by extension CAD. BOC Governor Macklem spoke but provided no fresh hawkish catalyst. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: Brent below $99/bbl (range $96.50–99.45), WTI $90–92 — oil softening on deal optimism
IEA cuts 2026 oil demand forecast by 80k BPD — structural bearish for oil/CAD
US crude private inventories +6.1mln vs -1.3mln exp — surprise build bearish for oil
Energy sector clear laggard in equities — risk-on does not benefit CAD when oil falls
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

CHF.tone: Haven Bid Fading in Risk-On — SNB Schlegel Speaks Today; Oil Decline Mild Support
CHF.summary: CHF is Neutral today. The Franc's haven bid is fading as risk appetite improves on US-Iran peace talk progress. The risk-on environment reduces demand for safe havens, and the SNB's intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength. Oil's decline provides mild support to Switzerland as a net energy importer, but this is insufficient to generate a directional bias. SNB Chairman Schlegel speaks today — the key event for CHF this week. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment.
drivers: Haven bid fading as risk-on conditions persist — US-Iran deal optimism reduces CHF demand
SNB Schlegel speaks today — key event for CHF; watch for any intervention signals
Oil decline provides mild support as Switzerland is a net energy importer
SNB intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength
Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment

NZD.tone: NZD G10 Outperformer — ANZ Revises RBNZ to Hikes Jul/Sep/Oct; Breman Speaks Today
NZD.summary: NZD is Bullish today and was the G10 outperformer in the London session. ANZ revised its RBNZ rate outlook to hikes in July, September and October — ANZ Chief Economist said it was 'fair to interpret the recent media appearances from Governor Breman as deliberate.' ANZ's Zollner added: 'We don't have a strong view on July versus September. But we do have a pretty strong view that hikes will come before our previous call of December.' RBNZ Governor Breman speaks today — any reinforcement of the hawkish stance would extend NZD strength. The combination of the RBNZ hawkish repricing and the risk-on environment is a powerful dual tailwind. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: ANZ revised RBNZ to hikes Jul/Sep/Oct — massive hawkish repricing, NZD G10 outperformer
ANZ Chief Economist: 'fair to interpret Breman's media appearances as deliberate' — hawkish signal
RBNZ Breman speaks today — hawkish reinforcement would extend NZD strength
Risk-on environment amplifies RBNZ hawkish story — dual tailwind
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED
